subtopics.title: REST API Deployment
subtopics.explanation: REST API deployment wraps trained models in web services that accept HTTP requests and return predictions. Key components: 1) API endpoints (POST /predict, GET /health), 2) Request/response validation (Pydantic models), 3) Model loading and inference, 4) Error handling, 5) Monitoring (metrics, logging). Frameworks: FastAPI (async, automatic docs), Flask (lightweight), Django (full-featured). Best practices: use async for I/O, add request/response caching, implement rate limiting, add authentication, monitor latency and throughput.
subtopics.example: POST /predict with JSON {'text': 'I love this product'} returns {'sentiment': 'positive', 'confidence': 0.95, 'latency_ms': 12.3}
subtopics.code:
from fastapi import FastAPI, HTTPException, BackgroundTasks
from pydantic import BaseModel, Field
from typing import List, Dict, Optional
import torch
import torch.nn as nn
import numpy as np
import time
import asyncio
from contextlib import asynccontextmanager
import logging
from prometheus_client import Counter, Histogram, generate_latest
import redis
import hashlib

# ============ Model Definition ============
class SentimentModel(nn.Module):
    def __init__(self, vocab_size=10000, embed_dim=128, num_classes=3):
        super().__init__()
        self.embedding = nn.Embedding(vocab_size, embed_dim)
        self.lstm = nn.LSTM(embed_dim, 64, batch_first=True, bidirectional=True)
        self.fc = nn.Linear(128, num_classes)
    
    def forward(self, x):
        embedded = self.embedding(x)
        lstm_out, _ = self.lstm(embedded)
        pooled = lstm_out.mean(dim=1)
        return self.fc(pooled)

# ============ Request/Response Models ============
class PredictRequest(BaseModel):
    text: str = Field(..., min_length=1, max_length=1000, description="Input text")
    max_length: Optional[int] = Field(100, ge=1, le=500, description="Max tokens")

class BatchPredictRequest(BaseModel):
    texts: List[str] = Field(..., min_items=1, max_items=100)
    max_length: Optional[int] = 100

class PredictResponse(BaseModel):
    sentiment: str
    confidence: float
    probabilities: Dict[str, float]
    latency_ms: float
    request_id: str

class BatchPredictResponse(BaseModel):
    predictions: List[PredictResponse]
    total_latency_ms: float
    batch_size: int

class HealthResponse(BaseModel):
    status: str
    model_loaded: bool
    device: str
    uptime_seconds: float
    requests_served: int

# ============ Metrics ============
request_count = Counter('model_requests_total', 'Total requests', ['endpoint', 'status'])
request_latency = Histogram('model_request_latency_seconds', 'Request latency', ['endpoint'])

# ============ Model Manager ============
class ModelManager:
    def __init__(self):
        self.model = None
        self.device = torch.device("cuda" if torch.cuda.is_available() else "cpu")
        self.sentiment_labels = {0: "negative", 1: "neutral", 2: "positive"}
        self.cache = redis.Redis(host='localhost', port=6379, decode_responses=True) if False else {}
        self.requests_served = 0
        self.start_time = time.time()
    
    def load_model(self):
        """Load model from disk"""
        self.model = SentimentModel()
        # In production: load trained weights
        # self.model.load_state_dict(torch.load('model.pth'))
        self.model.to(self.device)
        self.model.eval()
        logging.info(f"Model loaded on {self.device}")
    
    def preprocess(self, text: str, max_length: int = 100):
        """Convert text to tensor"""
        # Simplified tokenization
        tokens = [min(ord(c), 9999) for c in text[:max_length]]
        padded = tokens + [0] * (max_length - len(tokens))
        return torch.tensor([padded], dtype=torch.long).to(self.device)
    
    def predict(self, text: str, max_length: int = 100, request_id: str = None):
        """Single prediction with caching"""
        start_time = time.time()
        
        # Check cache
        cache_key = hashlib.md5(f"{text[:200]}_{max_length}".encode()).hexdigest()
        if cache_key in self.cache:
            result = self.cache[cache_key]
            result['cached'] = True
            return result
        
        # Inference
        with torch.no_grad():
            input_tensor = self.preprocess(text, max_length)
            output = self.model(input_tensor)
            probabilities = torch.softmax(output, dim=1)[0]
            pred_class = torch.argmax(probabilities).item()
        
        result = {
            'sentiment': self.sentiment_labels[pred_class],
            'confidence': probabilities[pred_class].item(),
            'probabilities': {
                self.sentiment_labels[i]: prob.item() 
                for i, prob in enumerate(probabilities)
            },
            'latency_ms': (time.time() - start_time) * 1000,
            'cached': False
        }
        
        # Cache result (TTL 1 hour)
        self.cache[cache_key] = result
        self.requests_served += 1
        
        return result

# ============ Rate Limiter ============
class RateLimiter:
    def __init__(self, requests_per_minute=60):
        self.requests_per_minute = requests_per_minute
        self.requests = {}
    
    def check(self, client_id: str) -> bool:
        now = time.time()
        window_start = now - 60
        
        if client_id not in self.requests:
            self.requests[client_id] = []
        
        # Clean old requests
        self.requests[client_id] = [t for t in self.requests[client_id] if t > window_start]
        
        if len(self.requests[client_id]) >= self.requests_per_minute:
            return False
        
        self.requests[client_id].append(now)
        return True

# ============ FastAPI Application ============
model_manager = ModelManager()
rate_limiter = RateLimiter(requests_per_minute=100)

@asynccontextmanager
async def lifespan(app: FastAPI):
    """Startup and shutdown events"""
    logging.info("Starting server...")
    model_manager.load_model()
    yield
    logging.info("Shutting down...")

app = FastAPI(
    title="ML Model Serving API",
    version="1.0.0",
    description="Sentiment analysis model deployment",
    lifespan=lifespan
)

@app.get("/health", response_model=HealthResponse, tags=["Health"])
async def health_check():
    """Health check endpoint for monitoring"""
    return HealthResponse(
        status="healthy" if model_manager.model else "unhealthy",
        model_loaded=model_manager.model is not None,
        device=str(model_manager.device),
        uptime_seconds=time.time() - model_manager.start_time,
        requests_served=model_manager.requests_served
    )

@app.get("/metrics", tags=["Monitoring"])
async def get_metrics():
    """Prometheus metrics endpoint"""
    return generate_latest()

@app.post("/predict", response_model=PredictResponse, tags=["Prediction"])
async def predict(request: PredictRequest, x_client_id: str = "default"):
    """Single prediction endpoint"""
    
    # Rate limiting
    if not rate_limiter.check(x_client_id):
        raise HTTPException(status_code=429, detail="Rate limit exceeded")
    
    # Track metrics
    request_count.labels(endpoint='/predict', status='start').inc()
    
    start_time = time.time()
    
    try:
        result = model_manager.predict(request.text, request.max_length)
        
        request_latency.labels(endpoint='/predict').observe(time.time() - start_time)
        request_count.labels(endpoint='/predict', status='success').inc()
        
        return PredictResponse(
            sentiment=result['sentiment'],
            confidence=result['confidence'],
            probabilities=result['probabilities'],
            latency_ms=result['latency_ms'],
            request_id=f"req_{int(time.time()*1000)}"
        )
    except Exception as e:
        request_count.labels(endpoint='/predict', status='error').inc()
        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=str(e))

@app.post("/predict/batch", response_model=BatchPredictResponse, tags=["Prediction"])
async def predict_batch(request: BatchPredictRequest):
    """Batch prediction endpoint"""
    start_time = time.time()
    
    try:
        predictions = []
        for text in request.texts:
            result = model_manager.predict(text, request.max_length)
            predictions.append(PredictResponse(
                sentiment=result['sentiment'],
                confidence=result['confidence'],
                probabilities=result['probabilities'],
                latency_ms=result['latency_ms'],
                request_id=f"batch_{int(time.time()*1000)}"
            ))
        
        return BatchPredictResponse(
            predictions=predictions,
            total_latency_ms=(time.time() - start_time) * 1000,
            batch_size=len(request.texts)
        )
    except Exception as e:
        raise HTTPException(status_code=500, detail=str(e))

# ============ Background Tasks ============
@app.post("/predict/async", tags=["Async"])
async def predict_async(request: PredictRequest, background_tasks: BackgroundTasks):
    """Async prediction with background processing"""
    request_id = f"async_{int(time.time()*1000)}"
    background_tasks.add_task(process_async_prediction, request, request_id)
    return {"request_id": request_id, "status": "processing"}

async def process_async_prediction(request: PredictRequest, request_id: str):
    """Process async prediction"""
    result = model_manager.predict(request.text, request.max_length)
    # Store result in database/queue
    logging.info(f"Async prediction {request_id} completed: {result['sentiment']}")

# ============ WebSocket Support ============
from fastapi import WebSocket

@app.websocket("/ws/predict")
async def websocket_predict(websocket: WebSocket):
    """WebSocket endpoint for real-time predictions"""
    await websocket.accept()
    try:
        while True:
            data = await websocket.receive_text()
            result = model_manager.predict(data)
            await websocket.send_json(result)
    except Exception as e:
        logging.error(f"WebSocket error: {e}")
        await websocket.close()

# ============ Demonstration ============
print("🔷 REST API Deployment")
print("=" * 60)

print("\n📊 API Endpoints:")
print("  • GET  /health      - Health check")
print("  • GET  /metrics     - Prometheus metrics")
print("  • POST /predict     - Single prediction")
print("  • POST /predict/batch - Batch prediction")
print("  • POST /predict/async - Async prediction")
print("  • WS   /ws/predict  - WebSocket streaming")

print("\n📊 Request/Response Example:")
print("-" * 40)
print("Request:")
print("  POST /predict")
print("  Content-Type: application/json")
print("  {")
print('    "text": "This product is amazing!",')
print('    "max_length": 100')
print("  }")
print("\nResponse:")
print("  {")
print('    "sentiment": "positive",')
print('    "confidence": 0.95,')
print('    "latency_ms": 12.34,')
print('    "request_id": "req_1700000000000"')
print("  }")

print("\n✅ REST API Best Practices:")
print("  • Use Pydantic for request/response validation")
print("  • Implement health checks for monitoring")
print("  • Add rate limiting to prevent abuse")
print("  • Use request IDs for tracing")
print("  • Implement response caching")
print("  • Add Prometheus metrics for monitoring")
print("  • Use async for I/O operations")
print("  • Add proper error handling and logging")
print("  • Use WebSockets for real-time streaming")

print("\n💡 Deployment Options:")
print("  • Local: uvicorn main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000")
print("  • Production: Gunicorn + Uvicorn workers")
print("  • Cloud: AWS Lambda, Google Cloud Run, Azure Functions")
print("  • Kubernetes: Deploy as microservice")
print("  • Edge: TensorFlow.js, ONNX.js for browser")

subtopics.title: Docker Containerization
subtopics.explanation: Docker packages ML models and dependencies into lightweight, portable containers that run consistently across environments. Benefits: 1) Reproducibility (same environment everywhere), 2) Isolation (no dependency conflicts), 3) Scalability (easy horizontal scaling), 4) Portability (run anywhere Docker runs). Key components: Dockerfile (build instructions), image (read-only template), container (running instance). Multi-stage builds reduce image size. Best practices: use slim base images, layer caching, non-root user, health checks, proper logging.
subtopics.example: Docker image containing Python 3.9, PyTorch, FastAPI, and trained model. Image size ~500MB. Run container: docker run -p 8000:8000 sentiment-api. Same image runs on laptop, server, or cloud.
subtopics.code:
# ============ Dockerfile ============
"""
# Multi-stage build for smaller image size
# Stage 1: Builder
FROM python:3.9-slim as builder

WORKDIR /app

# Install build dependencies
RUN apt-get update && apt-get install -y --no-install-recommends \
    gcc \
    && rm -rf /var/lib/apt/lists/*

# Copy requirements and install packages
COPY requirements.txt .
RUN pip install --user --no-cache-dir -r requirements.txt

# Stage 2: Runtime
FROM python:3.9-slim

# Create non-root user
RUN groupadd -r mluser && useradd -r -g mluser mluser

WORKDIR /app

# Copy installed packages from builder
COPY --from=builder /root/.local /home/mluser/.local

# Copy application code
COPY --chown=mluser:mluser . .

# Switch to non-root user
USER mluser

# Environment variables
ENV PYTHONPATH=/home/mluser/.local/lib/python3.9/site-packages
ENV MODEL_PATH=/app/models/model.pth

# Expose port
EXPOSE 8000

# Health check
HEALTHCHECK --interval=30s --timeout=3s --start-period=5s --retries=3 \
  CMD python -c "import requests; requests.get('http://localhost:8000/health')"

# Run application
CMD ["uvicorn", "main:app", "--host", "0.0.0.0", "--port", "8000"]
"""

# ============ requirements.txt ============
"""
fastapi==0.104.1
uvicorn[standard]==0.24.0
pydantic==2.5.0
torch==2.1.0
numpy==1.24.3
scikit-learn==1.3.0
redis==5.0.1
prometheus-client==0.19.0
python-multipart==0.0.6
httpx==0.25.1
"""

# ============ docker-compose.yml ============
"""
version: '3.8'

services:
  api:
    build: .
    ports:
      - "8000:8000"
    environment:
      - REDIS_HOST=redis
      - MODEL_PATH=/app/models/model.pth
    volumes:
      - ./models:/app/models
    depends_on:
      - redis
    networks:
      - ml-network
    deploy:
      replicas: 3
      resources:
        limits:
          cpus: '1'
          memory: 2G
    healthcheck:
      test: ["CMD", "curl", "-f", "http://localhost:8000/health"]
      interval: 30s
      timeout: 10s
      retries: 3

  redis:
    image: redis:7-alpine
    ports:
      - "6379:6379"
    networks:
      - ml-network
    volumes:
      - redis-data:/data

  nginx:
    image: nginx:alpine
    ports:
      - "80:80"
    volumes:
      - ./nginx.conf:/etc/nginx/nginx.conf
    depends_on:
      - api
    networks:
      - ml-network

  prometheus:
    image: prom/prometheus
    ports:
      - "9090:9090"
    volumes:
      - ./prometheus.yml:/etc/prometheus/prometheus.yml
    networks:
      - ml-network

networks:
  ml-network:
    driver: bridge

volumes:
  redis-data:
"""

# ============ nginx.conf (Load Balancer) ============
"""
events {}

http {
    upstream ml_backend {
        least_conn;
        server api:8000 max_fails=3 fail_timeout=30s;
        server api:8000;
        server api:8000;
    }

    server {
        listen 80;
        
        location / {
            proxy_pass http://ml_backend;
            proxy_set_header Host $host;
            proxy_set_header X-Real-IP $remote_addr;
            proxy_set_header X-Forwarded-For $proxy_add_x_forwarded_for;
            proxy_read_timeout 60s;
        }
        
        location /metrics {
            proxy_pass http://ml_backend/metrics;
        }
    }
}
"""

# ============ prometheus.yml ============
"""
global:
  scrape_interval: 15s

scrape_configs:
  - job_name: 'ml-api'
    static_configs:
      - targets: ['api:8000']
    metrics_path: /metrics
"""

# ============ Makefile (Build Commands) ============
"""
.PHONY: build run test clean deploy

build:
	docker build -t sentiment-api:latest .

run:
	docker run -p 8000:8000 --name sentiment-api sentiment-api:latest

run-prod:
	docker-compose up -d

stop:
	docker-compose down

test:
	pytest tests/

logs:
	docker-compose logs -f api

clean:
	docker system prune -f

deploy:
	docker tag sentiment-api:latest registry.example.com/sentiment-api:${TAG}
	docker push registry.example.com/sentiment-api:${TAG}

shell:
	docker exec -it sentiment-api /bin/bash
"""

# ============ kubernetes-deployment.yaml ============
"""
apiVersion: apps/v1
kind: Deployment
metadata:
  name: sentiment-api
  labels:
    app: sentiment-api
spec:
  replicas: 3
  selector:
    matchLabels:
      app: sentiment-api
  template:
    metadata:
      labels:
        app: sentiment-api
    spec:
      containers:
      - name: api
        image: sentiment-api:latest
        ports:
        - containerPort: 8000
        env:
        - name: MODEL_PATH
          value: "/app/models/model.pth"
        - name: REDIS_HOST
          value: "redis-service"
        resources:
          requests:
            memory: "1Gi"
            cpu: "500m"
          limits:
            memory: "2Gi"
            cpu: "1000m"
        livenessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health
            port: 8000
          initialDelaySeconds: 30
          periodSeconds: 10
        readinessProbe:
          httpGet:
            path: /health
            port: 8000
          initialDelaySeconds: 5
          periodSeconds: 5
---
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: sentiment-api-service
spec:
  selector:
    app: sentiment-api
  ports:
  - port: 80
    targetPort: 8000
  type: LoadBalancer
---
apiVersion: autoscaling/v2
kind: HorizontalPodAutoscaler
metadata:
  name: sentiment-api-hpa
spec:
  scaleTargetRef:
    apiVersion: apps/v1
    kind: Deployment
    name: sentiment-api
  minReplicas: 2
  maxReplicas: 10
  metrics:
  - type: Resource
    resource:
      name: cpu
      target:
        type: Utilization
        averageUtilization: 70
  - type: Resource
    resource:
      name: memory
      target:
        type: Utilization
        averageUtilization: 80
"""

# ============ .dockerignore ============
"""
__pycache__
*.pyc
*.pyo
*.pyd
.Python
*.so
*.egg
*.egg-info
dist
build
.git
.gitignore
*.md
.env
.venv
venv
__pycache__/
*.pkl
*.pt
*.pth
.DS_Store
"""

# ============ Entrypoint Script ============
"""
#!/bin/bash
# entrypoint.sh

echo "Starting model server..."

# Wait for Redis
while ! nc -z $REDIS_HOST 6379; do
  echo "Waiting for Redis..."
  sleep 1
done

echo "Redis available, starting API..."

# Start the application
exec uvicorn main:app --host 0.0.0.0 --port 8000 --workers 4
"""

# Demonstration
print("\n🔷 Docker Containerization")
print("=" * 60)

print("\n📊 Docker Commands:")
print("-" * 40)
print("  # Build image")
print("  docker build -t sentiment-api:latest .")
print("\n  # Run container")
print("  docker run -p 8000:8000 sentiment-api:latest")
print("\n  # Run with environment variables")
print("  docker run -p 8000:8000 -e MODEL_PATH=/models/model.pth sentiment-api:latest")
print("\n  # Run in background")
print("  docker run -d --name sentiment-api -p 8000:8000 sentiment-api:latest")
print("\n  # View logs")
print("  docker logs -f sentiment-api")
print("\n  # Stop container")
print("  docker stop sentiment-api")
print("\n  # Remove container")
print("  docker rm sentiment-api")
print("\n  # Run with Docker Compose")
print("  docker-compose up -d")
print("\n  # Scale service")
print("  docker-compose up -d --scale api=3")

print("\n📊 Dockerfile Best Practices:")
print("  • Use multi-stage builds for smaller images")
print("  • Use slim/alpine base images")
print("  • Run as non-root user")
print("  • Leverage layer caching (copy requirements first)")
print("  • Add health checks")
print("  • Use .dockerignore to exclude files")
print("  • Set appropriate resource limits")
print("  • Use environment variables for configuration")

print("\n📊 Docker Compose Services:")
print("  • API: Model serving service (scalable)")
print("  • Redis: Caching layer")
print("  • Nginx: Load balancer")
print("  • Prometheus: Metrics collection")

print("\n📊 Kubernetes Features:")
print("  • Horizontal Pod Autoscaling (CPU/Memory)")
print("  • Rolling updates and rollbacks")
print("  • Service discovery and load balancing")
print("  • Resource limits and requests")
print("  • Liveness and readiness probes")
print("  • ConfigMaps and Secrets for configuration")

print("\n✅ Containerization Benefits:")
print("  • Consistency: Same environment everywhere")
print("  • Isolation: No dependency conflicts")
print("  • Portability: Run on any Docker host")
print("  • Scalability: Easy horizontal scaling")
print("  • Versioning: Image version control")
print("  • CI/CD: Seamless integration")

print("\n💡 Production Deployment Checklist:")
print("  □ Use multi-stage Docker builds")
print("  □ Implement health checks")
print("  □ Add logging and monitoring")
print("  □ Configure resource limits")
print("  □ Use secrets for credentials")
print("  □ Enable HTTPS/SSL")
print("  □ Set up auto-scaling")
print("  □ Implement CI/CD pipeline")
print("  □ Use blue-green or canary deployments")
print("  □ Backup model versions")
